subject: Computer Networking
title: Aloha and Early Wireless
description: One-minute skill: Aloha and Early Wireless.

hook.text: In 1970s Hawaii, researchers wanted to connect island campuses without cables. They used radio waves.

buildup.text: ALOHAnet was one of the first wireless data networks. Stations transmitted whenever they had data to send.

discovery.text: If two stations transmitted at once, their signals collided. A simple retry mechanism handled the conflict.

twist.text: This collision-and-retry concept became the basis of Ethernet. Bob Metcalfe adapted it for wired networks.

climax.text: From a small island experiment, the ideas behind ALOHAnet shaped how billions of devices share networks today.

punchline.text: Wi-Fi and Ethernet both trace back to a Hawaiian experiment.

quiz.question: What networking concept originated from ALOHAnet?
